data.title: Google Cloud customers spend about 50% more than commitments, Kurian says
data.description: According to CNBC, Google Cloud chief Thomas Kurian said existing customers are spending roughly 50% more than they commit to when they sign up for the company's products, helping drive second-quarter cloud growth. He said the higher spending reflects the strength of Google Cloud's product lineup and go-to-market execution, and noted that the trend is showing up in both top-line and operating income growth.

data.title: Oil Tops $100 as Houthi Red Sea Attacks Open New Front in Middle East Conflict
data.description:
Bloomberg reported that oil topped $100 a barrel for the first time in two months after Iran-backed Houthi militants said they attacked two Saudi Arabian tankers in the Red Sea, escalating the Middle East conflict and threatening deeper supply disruptions.

The Yemen-based Houthis said they fired missiles and drones at the vessels to enforce a blockade of Saudi ports announced this week. The attacks open a new front in the conflict, with the Iran war already snarling traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

Targeting the Red Sea imperils the critical detour route that the Saudis used to keep oil barrels flowing amid the Hormuz disruptions. Exports from major producer Kazakhstan are also endangered by the Russia-Ukraine war, and global oil inventories are depleted by months of conflict.

Brent futures have surged more than 30% since the start of the month to trade near the highest since late May. Iran has struck vessels in Hormuz while the US has bombarded the Islamic Republic over the past 12 days and restored a blockade of its ports.

Rapidan Energy Group president Bob McNally, a former White House official, said round 2 of the military conflict is going to be broader than round 1, with risks to both shipping and energy infrastructure.

U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to hit Iranian bridges and power plants and reiterated threats to strike a suspected nuclear site. Washington and Tehran have both played down the prospect of peace talks.

Some vessels are already avoiding the Red Sea route, with Indian state firms and other Asian buyers contemplating a switch to longer, costlier routes via the Suez Canal. Kazakhstan is being forced to stop piping crude to its main export terminal on Russia's Black Sea coast following drone attacks on tankers.

data.title: Google Discloses $811 Billion in Future Contract Spending Commitments
data.description:
Google said in a quarterly filing that its future contract spending commitments reached $811 billion by the end of June, up nearly $500 billion from three months earlier. According to Odaily, the amount is disclosed separately from Google’s capital expenditure budget and reflects purchases committed under supply agreements and outstanding purchase orders.

About $200.7 billion of the commitments are short-term purchases. The commitments cover chips, data centers, electricity, inventory, and content licenses. Alphabet said on Wednesday that it expects to spend about $195 billion to $205 billion in capital expenditures this year.

data.title: Abraxas Capital Transfers $223.53 Million to Exchanges in 7 Hours
data.description: Onchain Lens monitoring shows Abraxas Capital transferred approximately $223.53 million in assets to multiple cryptocurrency exchanges over the past 7 hours. According to NS3.AI, the firm also withdrew approximately $26.42 million in assets from exchanges during the same period.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia Says Black Sea Waters Are Unsafe for Ships
data.description: Russia warned that its Black Sea waters are unsafe for navigation after Ukraine stepped up attacks on ships, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | Cleveland-Cliffs Promotes CFO Celso Goncalves to President
data.description: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. promoted Chief Financial Officer Celso Goncalves to president, elevating the son of the steelmaker’s chief executive officer, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Summit Updates Ivonescimab OS Data and US FDA Review Timing
data.description:
Summit said the overall survival hazard ratio for ivonescimab plus chemotherapy in the HARMONi study fell to 0.76 in the all-treated population and in the Western subgroup, based on data through June 2026. The company said it has submitted the result to the US FDA. Jiemian News reported that the update extends the follow-up period and brings the Western subgroup result in line with the benefit seen in China’s phase 3 study.

HARMONi is a global phase 3 trial of ivonescimab plus chemotherapy as second-line treatment for EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer. In China, Akeso’s HARMONi-A phase 3 trial previously reported statistically positive results on both progression-free survival and overall survival, with PFS HR at 0.46 and OS HR at 0.74. Ivonescimab was first approved in mainland China in May 2024. Summit had previously reported OS HR of 0.79 for the all-treated population and 0.98 for the Western subgroup in an earlier readout, before the latest update lowered both figures to 0.76.

Summit filed the US marketing application for this indication in the fourth quarter of 2025, and the FDA target action date is November 14, 2026. Akeso said 2025 revenue was 3.056 billion yuan, including about 3.033 billion yuan in commercial sales, up 51% year on year, driven mainly by cadonilimab and ivonescimab. The company said ivonescimab has also been approved in mainland China for first-line PD-L1-positive non-small cell lung cancer, while its application for first-line advanced squamous non-small cell lung cancer has been accepted.

data.title: Russia’s Oil and Gas Tax Revenue Expected to Rise 60% in July
data.description: According to Reuters calculations, Russia’s state oil and gas tax revenue is expected to rise 60% year over year in July to 1.3 trillion rubles, benefiting from higher global oil prices.

data.title: STOCKS | Google Falls Over 7% After Raising This Year's CapEx Forecast
data.description: Google fell over 7% on Thursday after lifting its capital expenditure forecast for this year to $195 billion-$205 billion from $190 billion. According to NS3.AI, Meta, Microsoft, and Amazon are set to report next week after the four companies previously disclosed up to $725 billion in planned AI development spending this year. Returns on these investments remain unclear.

data.title: STOCKS | Alphabet Future Spending Commitment Rises to $811 Billion
data.description: According to market news, Alphabet's future spending commitment has surged to $811 billion.

data.title: CZ Reflects on BitMEX Closure, Praises Arthur Hayes for Orderly Wind-Down
data.description:
Binance founder and former CEO Changpeng Zhao (CZ) said in a post on X that it is sad to see BitMEX go, noting that the exchange pioneered 100x perpetuals in crypto back in 2014 when delivery futures existed before then, making Fridays hectic.

CZ said BitMEX's BTC-only deposits, single-chain operations, and once-daily withdrawals through a multi-sig wallet were constraints that seemed inconvenient but kept the exchange unhacked throughout its history.

CZ noted that all four founders pled guilty to BSA violations a month before trial after 18 months of fighting, receiving $10 million fines each and home confinement, with no one going to prison.

However, CZ said their business did not survive the war on crypto from the Biden administration. CZ added that the wind-down appears orderly and users can withdraw their assets, expressing respect to Arthur Hayes.

data.title: Egypt Sees Path to Easing Iran-US Tensions, Says Cairo Is Pushing Diplomacy
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing Bloomberg, Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdel Aati said on Thursday, July 23, that after about two weeks of tit-for-tat strikes between Iran and the United States, Egypt sees a path to easing tensions and resuming peace talks. He said Cairo is working with the main mediators to push for a diplomatic solution, and that Egypt is in daily contact with the warring parties, Gulf states, Pakistan and Turkey as it seeks to bring them back to the terms set out in the June 15 memorandum of understanding signed by the United States and Iran.

Speaking on the sidelines of an ASEAN meeting in the Philippines, Abdel Aati said he was due to meet Oman’s foreign minister on Thursday. He said a specific proposal is under discussion that he hopes will help de-escalate the situation, and added that it still needs further discussion between Oman and Iran and is related to freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. Abdel Aati said talks can resume if both sides have the political will, to implement the June interim agreement and reach a final deal.

data.title: Greentech Technology Offers Raised To HK$0.55 Per Share
data.description:
Greentech Technology (0195), which has been suspended for a long period, said tonight that one of its offerors, Argyle Street Management (ASM)-led company, has raised its offer price by 37.5% to HK$0.55 from HK$0.45. The previous increase, from HK$0.25, was announced only 10 days ago by another offeror, High Energy Environment (Hong Kong), according to Ming Pao.

The partial offer remains unchanged at 230 million shares. After the price increase, the deal is valued at about HK$126.5 million and will be funded from internal financial resources.

data.title: Dow Says Middle East Tensions Could Lift Prices, Boost Guidance
data.description: Dow Inc. said persistent tensions in the Middle East could lift prices for plastics and packaging without increasing its material costs, improving profits and potentially adding upside to third-quarter guidance, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Teck Second-Quarter Earnings Triple on Higher Copper Prices
data.description: Canadian miner Teck Resources Ltd. said second-quarter earnings tripled as copper prices advanced, while the company reaffirmed output guidance, including for its flagship Quebrada Blanca mine in Chile, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Binance Partners With STOP THE TRAFFIK to Disrupt Human Trafficking Financial Flows
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, introducing Binance’s partnership with STOP THE TRAFFIK and outlining efforts to strengthen the fight against human trafficking and modern slavery by targeting the financial flows linked to exploitation. The article says human trafficking generated an estimated $528.5 billion in illicit proceeds in 2025, with those funds moving through financial systems that criminals attempt to exploit, including cryptocurrency. It also notes that more than 49.6 million people are living in situations of modern slavery today, and that the real figure is likely far higher. According to the latest NASDAQ 2026 Global Financial Crime Report, human trafficking generated an estimated $528.5 billion in illicit proceeds in 2025, growing 23.5% between 2023 and 2025. Binance says the partnership makes it the first crypto company to join forces with STOP THE TRAFFIK, a global non-profit focused on preventing human trafficking and modern slavery through intelligence-led action. The collaboration is intended to combine blockchain expertise with anti-trafficking intelligence to monitor and disrupt these flows, while also supporting Binance’s compliance, investigations, and public-private collaboration efforts worldwide.

The article says STOP THE TRAFFIK contributes experience gained through work with financial institutions, technology companies, NGOs, and law enforcement across the public, private, and non-profit sectors. Together, the two organizations aim to improve intelligence sharing, strengthen awareness, and identify emerging typologies tied to exploitation. Binance Chief Compliance Officer Noah Perlman said financial crime prevention must continue to evolve to address real-world harms connected to illicit finance, including human trafficking and related exploitation. He said the partnership will strengthen Binance’s understanding of these threats and improve its ability to contribute to efforts that protect vulnerable communities. Nick Dale, STOP THE TRAFFIK’s Director of Intelligence and Prevention, said traffickers depend on financial systems to launder money and increasingly use cryptocurrency to stay ahead of investigators. He added that the intelligence shared through the partnership can help target traffickers’ finances, disrupt operations, and reduce harm to millions of people globally. The collaboration will also include training and ongoing engagement on emerging risk indicators and typologies. The article adds that Binance’s broader efforts include working with expert organizations, law enforcement, regulators, and industry stakeholders. In 2025, Binance supported law enforcement in the takedown of Kidflix, one of the largest child exploitation platforms, and has assisted authorities on more than 313,000 law enforcement requests worldwide. Funds recovered or frozen with Binance’s support have surpassed $1 billion.

data.title: White House Official Says Trump's Tech Adviser Has Been Briefed on OpenAI Agent Runaway Incident
data.description: According to Jin10, a White House official said U.S. President Donald Trump's technology adviser, Kratsios, has been informed of OpenAI's agent runaway incident and is monitoring developments.

data.title: STOCKS | Nasdaq Loss Widens to 2%
data.description: According to Jin10, the Nasdaq's decline widened to 2%.

data.title: U.S. House Passes Iran War Powers Resolution
data.description: According to market sources, the U.S. House of Representatives passed an Iran War Powers Act resolution.

data.title: Telehealth Firms Urge FDA to Legalize Peptides With Guardrails
data.description:
Telehealth companies including Hims & Hers Health Inc. and Noom Inc. urged an expert panel to recommend legalizing a handful of popular peptides while adding stricter oversight and a system to report side effects, according to Bloomberg.

The companies want the U.S. Food and Drug Administration to allow the peptides under a regulated framework.
